text.title: Projections and EventBus
text.summary: OrderDetailsProjection handlers, simple EventBus for routing events to projections, and integration with command handlers.

rule.statement: Projection handlers must be idempotent — called with the same event twice must produce the same read-model state as called once.
rule.rationale: At-least-once event delivery guarantees duplicates will occur, especially during replay. Non-idempotent projections create corrupted read models on retry.

example.code:
class OrderDetailsProjection: def __init__(self, session): self._session = session
async def handle_order_created(self, event: OrderCreated) -> None: await self._session.execute( text(""" INSERT INTO order_details (order_id, customer_id, status, created_at) VALUES (:oid, :cid, 'draft', :occ) ON CONFLICT (order_id) DO NOTHING """), {"oid": str(event.order_id), "cid": str(event.customer_id), "occ": event.occurred_at}, )
async def handle_order_placed(self, event: OrderPlaced) -> None: await self._session.execute( text("UPDATE order_details SET status='placed', placed_at=:occ WHERE order_id=:oid"), {"oid": str(event.order_id), "occ": event.occurred_at}, )
example.note: ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING makes creation idempotent. UPDATE on status is naturally idempotent (setting the same value twice is harmless).
